snapshot.scope: Manual review binds public and documentation surfaces, control manifests, verifier code, and shared executable schemas. Focused automated gates execute current implementation and test evidence on every run.

reviewers.independence: Independent read-only review covered the exact documentation-governance candidate. It accepted retirement of two historical audits, current-state ownership in Playbook and acceptance, schema-v3 compact semantic locks with all 1,108 IDs preserved, unchanged phase and acceptance states, net-lower budgets, and a safe non-durable Home draft-replacement contract that exposes no raw provider, Pi, tool, reasoning, citation, credential, or error payload. The reviewer made no file edits.

dimensions.summary: The router and document map own the current library; two historical non-normative audits are removed without live references, while Git retains their rationale.
PRD, subject Owners, Playbook, acceptance, decisions, API, UI, Job and Pi agree on current-state ownership and the safe Home draft boundary.
All 1,108 claim IDs remain independently locked, multiline phase items are fully digested, and every phase, Requirement and Exit state remains unchanged.
Agents receive a smaller routed entry pack, stable Owners retain durable rules, and Playbook plus acceptance own current executable inventory and open work.
The library is reduced to 55 Markdown files, entry reading is 3,362 of 3,700 words, and governed Markdown is 1,112,939 of 1,210,000 bytes with no budget increase.

verification.evidence: The final exact candidate passes documentation, decisions, UI, independent review, TRC-001 through TRC-005, CON-001 through CON-004, dependency, fixture, boundary, no-telemetry, source-artifact and LEN-001 through LEN-005 gates.
The unchanged product code passes 80 test files plus one skipped file, with 843 passing tests and three explicitly skipped tests.
Leanness gates pass with 55 Markdown files, 3,362 of 3,700 entry words, 4,522 trace lines, 224,497 of 250,000 trace bytes and 1,112,939 of 1,210,000 governed Markdown bytes.

verification.command: node scripts/verify/independent-documentation-review.mjs --print-snapshot && explicit status and semantic-ledger comparison
verification.evidence: The reviewed 107-file governed snapshot is 2e7652b2db5a204837ea5626cb3d0c2d944c3291919161e68f4e647a29a751e5; current and locked ledgers contain 1,108 claims, and phase, Requirement and Exit states are unchanged.

coordination.taskTitle: Documentation governance consolidation and safe Home draft streaming
coordination.compatibility: This candidate changes documentation governance and defines a planned safe streaming boundary only. It does not change product code, executable behavior, phase state, Requirement state, Exit state, or packageability evidence.
coordination.acknowledgement: Product Planning completed the user-approved three-stage documentation consolidation in one isolated worktree; independent read-only review accepted all five dimensions and the final gates bind the exact current snapshot.

nonBlockingRisks: The draft_replace protocol is a planned contract with no executable evidence; PIGE-PI-005 and E3.09 remain planned and E6.01 remains partial.

Implementation must consume the typed already-parsed terminal answer snapshot and must never expose raw partial JSON, provider deltas, Pi events, reasoning, tool arguments, citations, grounding, identifiers, credentials, or raw errors.
Sender, request, client-turn, conversation, Job, user-event, monotonic-sequence, coalescing and cancellation tests remain required before streaming can be claimed executable.
Draft loss on crash or reconnect is intentional because drafts are non-durable; the UI must retain truthful pending or recovered durable state without replaying provisional text.
External or manual consumers of the semantic manifest must update for schema-v3 base64url digests even though repository gates are migrated.
Current implementation and open-work truth now depends on disciplined Playbook and acceptance updates; stable subject contracts must not regrow rolling status inventories.
Historical rationale for the two reclaimed audit files remains available through Git history rather than the active documentation library.
